title: The public record opens before the strongest proof does
summary: The chronology no longer starts in a vague way. The ledger now shows a visible pre-2013 bridge, then the denser first-party release trail where the chronicle becomes materially stronger.

entries.title: Open Virtualization Alliance overview by Bloombase
entries.summary: Useful as a bridge source: it proves Bloombase was already visible in secure virtualization discourse before the stronger 2013 company-hosted releases.

entries.title: Bloombase joins OpenStack community
entries.summary: The public founder-era chronology becomes direct here: Sean Xiang is named in the company-hosted release trail that anchors the chronicle more confidently.

entries.title: EMC Technology Partner Program
entries.summary: Shows the founder chapter immediately entering interoperability and infrastructure credibility territory rather than reading like a generic startup launch story.

title: Enterprise trust becomes a repeated pattern, not a single logo moment
summary: Once the company is public, the archive works best when readers can see the rhythm of alliances, certifications, and HSM / cloud adjacencies across multiple years.

stats.label: Direct or partner-hosted milestones in the arc
Distinct trust surfaces: platforms, HSMs, enterprise validation

entries.title: Dell Technology Partner certification
entries.summary: One of the early signs that the company is building trust by passing through enterprise partner programs, not just by publishing product claims.

entries.title: Ultra Electronics AEP Keyper HSM interoperability
entries.summary: Deepens the key-custody and HSM thread that gives the middle years real infrastructure texture instead of a generic alliance wall.

entries.title: Marvell LiquidSecurity Network HSM partnership
entries.summary: A newly captured partner-hosted URL thickens the 2019 chronology between Azure Marketplace and nCipher, making the trust layer feel continuous rather than sparse.

title: The latest chapter reads better when it feels like continuity, not reinvention
summary: The AI-era close is strongest when presented as the newest visible edge of a long security and infrastructure chronology rather than a disconnected pivot.

stats.label: Security continuity from storage era to AI era

entries.title: Rebuilding Bloombase with NVIDIA DPUs and GPUs
entries.summary: This is the cleanest statement of how the long data-at-rest story is being reframed for accelerated computing and AI infrastructure.

entries.title: Bloombase and Utimaco technology alliance for the AI era
entries.summary: A direct release surface that makes the closing chapter feel current, source-backed, and connected to the same trust infrastructure logic as the middle years.
